rawText:
James Rivera is a 58-year-old man brought in by his wife after he passed out briefly in the kitchen this morning. He says he blacked out for a few seconds and felt lightheaded before falling. He denies chest pain but reports palpitations with dizziness over the past week.

Medical history includes atrial fibrillation, coronary artery disease, and type 2 diabetes. He saw cardiology one month ago; no medication changes since then.

Current medications:
- Warfarin 7.5 mg once daily
- Aspirin 81 mg once daily (started by cardiology for CAD)
- Metformin 1000 mg twice daily
- Atorvastatin 40 mg at bedtime
- Metoprolol 50 mg twice daily

He has no headache, no weakness, and no slurred speech today. No recent bleeding. He manages medications independently.
